analysis.problem: Asteroid mining could destabilize global markets by creating an unregulated supply of space resources.
analysis.root_cause: Lack of international legal frameworks to govern space resource extraction and distribution.

proposed_actions.action: Establish a binding international treaty on space resource governance
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: At least 50% of major space-faring nations ratify the treaty within 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Create a transparent global registry for asteroid mining operations
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: All registered mining operations are publicly accessible and auditable within 3 years

evidence: We may be about to see an asteroid-mining gold rush. But here's why...: The risks of asteroid mining. As alluring as an impending space-age gold rush may sound, there are reasons for caution – not least the potential destabilisation of global markets.

analysis.generalization: This issue connects to the broader domain of cosmic resource management, where unregulated extraction could lead to market distortions, geopolitical tensions, and environmental risks across space and Earth.
analysis.summary: Asteroid mining could destabilize global markets due to lack of regulation, requiring international governance frameworks to prevent economic and geopolitical risks.

raw_items.title: Asteroid mining
raw_items.summary: Asteroid mining is the hypothetical and technically possible extraction of materials from asteroids and other minor planets, including near-Earth objects.Research missions focused on asteroid sample return, including Hayabusa, Hayabusa2, OSIRIS-REx, and Tianwen-2, illustrate the challenges of collecting ore from space using current technology. raw_items.summary: The risks of asteroid mining. As alluring as an impending space-age gold rush may sound, there are reasons for caution – not least the potential destabilisation of global markets.

raw_items.title: Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015
raw_items.summary: The Space Resource Exploration and Utilization Act of 2015 (H.R. 1508) was a bill (which never became law) introduced in the 114th Congress of the U.S. on March 19, 2015, by Rep. Bill Posey, Republican of Florida. It is identical to the Senate bill by the same name (S. 976).
